name: Pistachio Cake with White Chocolate Buttercream
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and lightly flour three 8-inch round baking pans.
2. In a bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, and baking soda. Set aside.
3. With an electric mixer on medium speed, beat butter until smooth.
4. Add sugar, vanilla, and almond extract to the butter; beat until fluffy.
5. Alternate adding flour mixture and buttermilk, beating on low speed just until combined.
6. Thoroughly wash beaters. In a large bowl, beat egg whites with an electric mixer until stiff peaks form (tips stand straight).
7. Gently fold beaten egg whites into the batter using a spatula.
8. Fold in pistachio nuts and orange peel.
9. Divide batter among prepared pans.
10. Bake for 30-35 minutes or until a toothpick inserted near the center comes out clean.
11. Cool cakes in pans on wire racks for 10 minutes.
12. Remove cakes from pans and cool completely on wire racks.
13. Frost with White Chocolate Buttercream.
14. Sprinkle remaining pistachio nuts on sides of cake.
15. Cover and store cake in refrigerator for up to 3 days. Let stand at room temperature for 30 minutes before serving.
WHITE CHOCOLATE BUTTERCREAM:
1. In a medium bowl, combine granulated sugar and all-purpose flour; add reserved egg yolks. Beat until combined.
2. Set aside.
3. Heat milk in a heavy saucepan to boiling.
4. Remove from heat. Gradually beat hot milk into the yolk mixture with a whisk; return to saucepan.
5. Cook over medium heat until bubbly, whisking constantly. Cook for 2 minutes more.
6. Remove from heat. Add chopped white chocolate, vanilla, and almond extract.
7. Allow to stand for 1 minute before stirring until smooth.
8. Transfer mixture to a bowl. Cover surface with plastic wrap; cool to room temperature.
9. In a large bowl, beat softened butter on medium speed until fluffy.
10. Add the cooled chocolate mixture in fourths, beating on low after each addition until blended.
